mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that restrictive monetary policy is transmitting into the interest-rate-sensitive housing sector, foreshadowing broader demand contraction and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% over 6 months while FEDFUNDS > 5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances when the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month slowdown in industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals capacity constraints and margin compression as the economy operates above sustainable output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations reset.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns follow less than 55 percent of triggered signals, or the rolling Rank IC between INDPRO change and forward returns falls below zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate sales and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS growth months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5%, the restrictive level of monetary policy materially slows credit creation and economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining real retail sales (RSAFS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms simultaneous deterioration in consumer spending capacity and labor income, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as recession probability rises.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 40 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals near-term supply-side weakness and inventory accumulation that pressures corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns follow less than 55 percent of month-over-month INDPRO contractions.
